_about: Cross-version constants for Derby Owners Club ROM specs. Facts here are true for all four mask-ROM versions unless a per-version spec overrides. Companion to ROM_ARCHITECTURE.md.

platform.cabinet: multiboard (server/master board + satellites; master runs the race sim)

addressRule.description: A byte at file offset X is pointed-to / executes at runtime address X + 0x0C020000. Ghidra static analysis loads at base 0x0C000000.
addressRule.warning: Baked data/code pointers in the ROM are RUNTIME values. Mixing the three frames (file, ghidra, runtime) is the top source of pointer-not-found confusion.

pointerConventions.literalPool: A baked runtime pointer in a function constant pool. Findable by scanning the ROM for the 4-byte LE runtime address. Located roster, breeding, food, strings, and the personality-interaction reader.
pointerConventions.computed: PC-relative or base+index access (mova @(disp,PC)); no standalone pointer exists. Scans return nothing even though the table is real. The race FPU tables and the personality table are reached this way; disassemble the reader to recover index math.

card.layout: 3 tracks x 69 bytes, stored reversed per track
card.note: No whole-card checksum in the payload, so cards are freely editable and silently mis-editable. Field map in CARD_BYTE_MAP.md. US/World cards are full-stat (SEGABEF0 marker); JP cards are identity/pedigree only with stats held cabinet-side (see _jp_re/JP_CARD_FORMAT_SPEC.md).

emulationNotes.rig: GDB-stub Flycast with Dynarec ON; software breakpoints do not fire (JIT). Memory reads/writes work. Use interpreter mode for code breakpoints.
emulationNotes.liveRamBase: Cart live RAM base means structs move per boot; locate by scanning, not hard-coded address.

tableSeparation.claim: CPU roster and breeding pool are SEPARATE tables, not one reused table.
tableSeparation.evidence: Roster (32-byte recs) and breeding sire/dam arrays (60-byte recs) are distinct regions with different record formats. They overlap only by NAME, both drawing from the same famous-Thoroughbred namespace. A shared name carries different stats in each table (e.g. Rev C Sunday Silence: racer dirt 255 vs breeding sire dirt 216).
tableSeparation.consequence: Reconcile breeding records by NAME, not index. Cross-version patching of diverged records is unsafe.
